transcript.text: Hello, Living in Lafayette, V-LOG, the 13th of May, 2013.
Oops, forgot to turn the light in the back off.
Well, I'll do that next time.
So let's see, what did I do today?
I got up a little bit late, like 9.30 or whatever,
Because I was up later, because I was writing later.
Finally got to sleep late.
But, yeah, I got up and then I tried to turn my little thing on,
My little GoPro.
But it was right when I was getting on tech support.
And it was turning on, and it said the percentages were going up.
So the guy's like, oh, yeah, once the percentage goes up, it'll work.
And I was like, all right.
And so we got off, and then I started talking to somebody on the phone
And packed up my stuff and went out to Boulder.
But I was cleaning up the house first,
So this thing was plugged in for like an hour and a half on my computer,
Because it doesn't come fully charged, so it was charging.
And then I put it in my car, and I plugged it up to the wall of my car to charge.
And I know the wall was working because my cell phone was plugged in there.
My cell phone, if you unplug it from anything, it dies.
I need to get a new cell phone, even though it's only a year old.
Well, at this point, almost a year and a half old.
But I guess I don't get another free cell phone for another couple of months,
So I have to just walk around with this thing plugged up and stuff.
But anyways, this thing was charging.
GoPro was charging.
I went into REI and got the helmet mount for it, because it didn't come with a helmet mount.
And then a little headgear mount so that I can use it for when I go running as well at REI.
And then I went into Pearl Street to Top Hat Cleaning Supply, Janitorial Supply,
And bought like $55 of rubber squeegees for washing windows.
And then, since I was in the neighborhood, I got a burrito at Illegal Pete's, even though it was like $7.50.
I just had been so long since I had that kind of a burrito.
And one-on-one.
And then I filmed Pearl Street, because I wanted to make a video about Boulder.
And then I drove all the way up to Batasta Basair to Mountain Bike,
And I tried to turn this thing on, and it wouldn't turn on.
And I was like, what the hell is wrong?
And I plugged it up to the computer, it wouldn't turn on.
And then, so I was like, screw this, I don't want to ride.
I want to get this thing fixed right away.
Maybe I can get it fixed with the tech support.
And then go back up there later on today.
So, because I couldn't even call tech support, there's no connection with the telephone.
Although I could, there was connection to like 911 services, so that's cool.
If you don't have phone connection, you still have some kind of magical connection to 911.
Anyways, I went home and got on tech support, and it found out this thing wasn't charged.
So, even though it was charging for an hour and a half on the computer, and then another two hours on the wall phone, on the wall, it wasn't charged.
But I plugged it up to the same jack it was on the computer before, and it had the little red light on, and said it was charging.
So, I was like, alright.

So, then I figured I was in town anyway, so I rode all over Boulder, or a lot of places in Boulder, filming, because I wanted to make a video about Boulder, until the end of the day, and then came home.
So, it was my mistake that I didn't make sure that it worked, and it's out of character as well.
I totally would have made sure it worked before leaving, but I was talking to somebody on the phone, and I just kind of grabbed my stuff and left, and forgot, and fucking bit me in the ass.
But I did manage to do some filming, so it wasn't a total waste, but I didn't have to go into Boulder today.
I didn't have to drive all that to the house that was just reserved, so that was a lot of waste of gas money.
But, yeah, my mom had the Kindle, and I forgot to bring my MP3.
I still don't know where it is.
I hope I can find it, because it's the only one I have right now.
Actually, I have another one, but anyway.
Okay, so, I borrowed my Kindle from my mom, and I pushed start on my little bike computer, because I wanted to even just record the trip I did around Boulder.
It would be cool to look at on the map, because it went all over the place.
But, yeah, and see what kind of, like, different time zones they passed through.
But anyway, you know, from Strava.
But I pushed play on this book I chose to read, that I went on my website, Kindle website, or Kindle books on my website, and chose one, Slave Narratives.
And pushed play, and then I did my whole day listening to it.
And then I came back to my car, and as I was strapping my bike to my car, the book ended.
So the book was the same exact length as my bike ride all around town.
So I did a whole book today.
Yeah, and then I came here, and it's like 9.16 now, so I want to get to sleep, because it's dark, and I want to get to sleep.
I want to, like, be a diurnalist.
I got up too late today.
I want to get up with the sun.
I want to get in the habit of that.
It's just, I kind of feel like I accomplished more in a day that way, rather than getting up late and then going to sleep late.
So I just want to be on this rollers and on the computer long enough to, just to drag the, all the videos I did onto my computer, and then onto my external.
Oh, whoops, I need to connect my external.
Maybe I can grab it without getting off.
Just to back it up, and, you know, put all those in a place, and then edit this one bike video about this table, and then, you know, upload this video log, and then take a shower and go to bed.
And tomorrow I'm going to go to Batasso, but not before I make sure that this fucking thing works.
So it'll be take four, but yeah, do Batasso, film it all, and then I get to make my video.
I'm so excited.
Oh, my God, you have no idea how excited I am to make, like, a quality video, you know?
Not like a classic Kyle Pounds push, record, and go video, but, like, you know, something that, with graphics and all that cool stuff, dude.
Because that's what, like, I didn't even ride when I went up there.
Like, I'm not even motivated to ride unless I'm filming it.
And, but it's going to make me very motivated to ride in all sorts of new places, and I want to make really good videos.
And there aren't that many good videos.
It's, you know, you type Batasso Preserve, and it's just amateurs pushing GoPro and just going a little bit.
You know, there's nobody who's really done it up, and so I want to do it.
And who knows?
Maybe, you know, make a few bucks here and there out of it.
So, yeah, that's that.
Okay.
Talk to you tomorrow.
Peace out.
